title: Hi, I'm Karthik - LLM Engineer
excerpt: LLM Engineer focused on how models behave in production—evals, tooling, and the boring work that keeps assistants reliable. This is my corner of the internet.
content:
# Hi, I'm Karthik

LLM Engineer. I care about how language models behave once they leave a notebook—latency, evaluation, guardrails, and the gap between a demo and something you'd actually ship.

This is my corner of the internet — a place where I write about what I'm learning, building, and occasionally staying up until 2 AM debugging.

---

## What I Do

My day-to-day sits at the intersection of ML systems and product:

- **Evals & quality** — defining what "good" means for a feature, measuring it, and catching regressions before users do
- **RAG & context** — retrieval design, chunking, and keeping answers grounded without turning the stack into spaghetti
- **Tooling & agents** — structured outputs, calling APIs safely, and knowing when *not* to give a model a hammer
- **Production hardening** — prompts are code; the infrastructure around models deserves the same discipline as any other service

I still think like someone who used to stress-test cloud configs for a living: assume failure, log what matters, and never trust a happy-path demo.

---

## Background

Before leaning into LLMs full-time, I spent a lot of cycles in **cloud security on AWS**—IAM, threat modeling, automation, and incident response. That shapes how I think about model deployments: least privilege, audit trails, and blast radius still matter when your compute is a tokenizer and a GPU.

---

## What I'm Building

Right now I'm focused on:

- Shipping and measuring model-backed features in real products—not just benchmarks in isolation
- Writing up lessons that were painful to learn and thinly documented elsewhere
- Staying connected through this blog and [DEV.to](https://dev.to/karthiblip)

---

— Karthik
